papers.title: Sleep disturbances and cognitive decline in elderly adults: A longitudinal cohort study
papers.abstract_summary: This 5-year prospective cohort study of 2,400 adults aged 65+ examined the relationship between sleep quality and cognitive decline. Poor sleep efficiency (<85%) was associated with 30% increased risk of dementia (HR 1.30, 95% CI 1.15-1.47). Sleep apnea treatment reduced cognitive decline by 40%.
key_findings: Poor sleep increases dementia risk by 30%
Sleep apnea treatment slows cognitive decline by 40%
7-8 hours sleep optimal; <6 or >9 hours associated with worse outcomes
Sleep fragmentation most predictive of decline

papers.title: Multifactorial fall prevention interventions in community-dwelling older adults: Systematic review and meta-analysis
papers.abstract_summary: Meta-analysis of 144 trials (79,193 participants) examining fall prevention interventions. Exercise programs reduced falls by 23% (RR 0.77, 95% CI 0.71-0.83). Home safety modifications reduced falls by 21%. Combined interventions most effective.
key_findings: Home safety modifications reduce falls by 21%
Tai Chi most effective exercise (38% reduction)
Vitamin D reduces falls if deficient
Multifactorial approaches superior to single interventions
clinical_applications: Prescribe Tai Chi or balance training

papers.title: Benzodiazepine use and fall risk in hospitalized older adults: A propensity-matched cohort study
papers.journal: Journal of the American Geriatrics Society
papers.abstract_summary: Study of 12,456 hospitalized adults aged 75+ examining benzodiazepine use and falls. Lorazepam use increased fall risk by 44% (OR 1.44, 95% CI 1.28-1.62). Risk highest in first 3 days of use. Dose-response relationship observed.
key_findings: Short-acting agents also risky in elderly
clinical_applications: Avoid benzodiazepines in elderly when possible
Use lowest effective dose if necessary

papers.title: Physical activity and prevention of cognitive decline: The Mayo Clinic Study of Aging
papers.abstract_summary: Population-based study of 1,604 cognitively normal adults aged 65+ followed for 8 years. Moderate physical activity (≥150 min/week) reduced MCI risk by 32%. Any activity better than none - even light activity showed benefit.
key_findings: Moderate exercise reduces MCI risk by 32%
Even light activity shows cognitive protection
Benefit seen across all APOE genotypes

papers.title: Frailty trajectories in older adults: Predictors and outcomes from the Women's Health and Aging Study
papers.abstract_summary: Longitudinal analysis of 1,002 women aged 65+ over 9 years. 38% developed frailty. Strongest predictors: low physical activity, cognitive impairment, depression, and weight loss. Frailty associated with 3x increased mortality and 5x increased nursing home admission.
key_findings: 38% of women developed frailty over 9 years
clinical_applications: Screen for frailty annually in elderly

papers.title: Anticholinergic burden and dementia risk: Systematic review of observational studies
papers.abstract_summary: Review of 21 studies (n=171,391) examining anticholinergic cognitive burden (ACB) and dementia. High ACB score (≥3) associated with 47% increased dementia risk (HR 1.47, 95% CI 1.33-1.62). Risk cumulative and reversible - discontinuation reduced risk.
key_findings: High anticholinergic burden increases dementia risk 47%
clinical_applications: Calculate ACB score for all elderly patients

papers.title: Home-based exercise program to prevent falls in high-risk elderly: Randomized controlled trial
papers.abstract_summary: RCT of 606 adults aged 70+ with fall history. Home-based balance and strength training reduced falls by 36% over 12 months (IRR 0.64, 95% CI 0.51-0.80). Program included 30 min exercises, 3x/week, with telephone support.

papers.title: Nutritional interventions for sarcopenia in older adults: Systematic review and meta-analysis
papers.abstract_summary: Meta-analysis of 37 RCTs examining protein supplementation and muscle mass. Protein intake >1.2g/kg/day increased muscle mass by 0.54kg (95% CI 0.18-0.90). Combined with resistance training: additional 0.94kg gain. Leucine-enriched supplements most effective.
key_findings: High protein intake (>1.2g/kg) increases muscle mass
Combined with exercise: nearly 1.5kg muscle gain

papers.title: Deprescribing in elderly patients with polypharmacy: Cluster randomized trial
papers.abstract_summary: Cluster RCT of 1,565 patients aged 70+ on ≥5 medications. Deprescribing intervention reduced medication count by 1.4 drugs (p<0.001). No increase in adverse events. Quality of life improved. Falls reduced by 18%.
key_findings: Deprescribing safely reduces medications by average 1.4
Most commonly stopped: PPIs, antihypertensives, hypoglycemics

papers.title: Cognitive stimulation therapy for mild cognitive impairment: Randomized controlled trial
papers.abstract_summary: RCT of 280 adults with MCI. 7-week cognitive stimulation program improved memory scores by 2.1 points (p<0.01) and quality of life. Benefits maintained at 6 months. Group-based approach most effective.
key_findings: Cognitive stimulation improves memory in MCI
clinical_applications: Offer cognitive stimulation to MCI patients
